channel.title: KiddieDaily — Science
channel.description: Kid-friendly science and nature news, bias-rated.

item.title: Another new Psyche mission flyby image of Mars
item.description: NASA released a composite image of Mars lit like a crescent as the Psyche mission flew by for a gravity assist. Watch a stunning time lapse of the whole flyby. The post Another new Psyche mission flyb

item.title: Science word of the day: Cryptology
item.description: Pronunciation: Krip-TAH'-leh-jee

item.title: New Species of Monkey Found in DR Congo Shows How Much There’s Left to Discover
item.description: When news reaches the public that a new species has been identified, the chances really are 9 times out of 10 that it’s some deep sea slug or a spider. From the Congo comes the story of the exce

item.title: Josh Kerr wants to run the fastest mile ever. This is the shoe Brooks built to help him do it.
item.description: We spoke with Danny Orr, senior director of product development at Brooks, to find out what it takes to build a track spike that can handle running a mile at more than 16 mph. The post Josh Kerr wants

item.title: A quasar breaks the record for most distant supermassive black hole
item.description: The Euclid space telescope discovery could help researchers understand how black holes grew so massive so quickly in the early universe.

item.title: James Webb telescope uses trippy Einstein prediction to probe the farthest reaches of the universe — Space photo of the week
item.description: The James Webb telescope peers through a warped bubble of space-time to study galaxies from the first billion years after the Big Bang.

item.title: ‘Dead’ coral reef found alive and thriving off West Africa
item.description: The long-lost mesophotic ecosystem near Benin may be part of an even bigger formation. The post ‘Dead’ coral reef found alive and thriving off West Africa appeared first on Popular Science

item.title: NASA’s Perseverance Rover Provides Sweeping View of Broom Point
item.description: Description This view looking back up at the outside lip of the 490-foot-tall (150-meter-tall) rim of Jezero Crater was taken by the Mastcam-Z instrument aboard NASA’s Perseverance on May 15, 2025, th

item.title: 'It's absolutely exciting!': Astronomers discover an atmosphere around an Earth-like planet in the habitable zone for first time ever
item.description: Astronomers have detected helium escaping from the super-Earth LHS 1140 b, providing the strongest evidence yet that a rocky planet in another star's habitable zone has retained an atmosphere for bill

item.title: Actively Steerable Femtosat Constellations for In-situ Exploration of Saturn’s Rings, Atmosphere, and Magnetosphere
item.description: Michael RubensteinNorthwestern University, Chicago We propose a mission to use ~10,000 actively steerable femtosats to map the ring composition, atmospheric composition and density, and the magnetic f

item.title: This “exotic weirdo” exoplanet has a rocky surface and an atmosphere
item.description: Astronomers found a helium atmosphere around a rocky exoplanet — a good sign for the search for life outside the solar system.

item.title: Reigning champion Argentina escapes with remarkable World Cup victory over Egypt
item.description: Egypt was leading late, up 2-0. The Argentinians looked beaten. But they fought and fought and fought. Scoring one goal, then another to equalize. And, finally, a third to advance to the quarterfinal.

item.title: NASA's Hubble spots a stellar sparkler for the Fourth of July
item.description: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope has captured a spectacular red, white, and blue view of one of the Milky Way's oldest star clusters to celebrate the nation's 250th anniversary. Hidden within the ancient

item.title: Scientists say Beefalo are all beef, no -alo. Breeders disagree
item.description: A whole-genome analysis of Beefalo, a hybrid bison-cattle breed, suggests very few individuals have any bison DNA at all, a new study reports.

item.title: Strange Spheres Washed Ashore on an Australian Beach. Authorities Say They're Probably 'Space Balls'—Empty Fuel Vessels From a Rocket
item.description: The shiny objects—each roughly twice the size of a basketball—began to appear on July 3. Authorities initially worried that they were hazardous but have since determined that they're likely safe bits

item.title: NASA Takes Flight For America’s 250th
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman leads a flyover featuring his personally owned Northrop F-5 Tiger during the Great American State Fair on July 4, 2026, on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. For 2

item.title: Neanderthals and modern humans may have shared culture 59,000 years ago in Turkey, study finds
item.description: Fossils, stone tools and seashells in Turkey show that Neanderthals and the Homo sapiens who moved in later had the same hunting strategies and symbolic traditions even without overlapping at the site

item.title: Alien life on nearby 'super Earth' much likelier than we thought, study claims
item.description: A recently discovered "super Earth" located around 25 light-years from our planet is not as massive as previously thought, raising the chances that it has the conditions to support life.

item.title: Examining Algal Blooms in Blue Mesa
item.description: Using satellite data, researchers connected harmful algal blooms with warm water and low water levels at one of Colorado’s largest reservoirs.

item.title: Euclid telescope discovers the 2 most ancient monster black holes in the universe ‪—‬ each brighter than a trillion suns
item.description: A collection of newfound objects discovered by the Euclid telescope more than doubles the number of known quasars from the universe's first billion years.
